OrganizationMembershipCreateRequest.description:
Body for `POST /v1/organizations/{org_id}/memberships`. Adds an
existing user to the organization as a member with the given
role.

To enroll a user who hasn't signed up yet, create an
`OrganizationInvitation` instead.

OrganizationMembershipUpdateRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /v1/organizations/{org_id}/memberships/{id}`. v0.2
supports changing the role; metadata edits are fire-and-forget.

OrganizationInvitation.description:
Pending invitation for a future member to join an
`Organization`. The recipient clicks `url`, lands on the Account
Portal pre-fill page, and either signs up (transferring the
ticket into a new `User` and creating the membership) or signs
in (creating the membership against the existing `User`).

Mirrors the application-level `Invitation` ticket convention
from PLAN §9.7 — `__authn_status=sign_up` is appended for
recipients who don't yet have a `User`.

role.description: Role `key` the recipient receives on accept.

role_name.description: Human-readable display name for `role` at the time of read.

inviter_user_id.description:
`User.id` of the org member who issued the invitation. `null`
for invitations created via BAPI without an `actor` user.

redirect_url.description:
Where the Account Portal returns the recipient after they
accept. Must match the redirect-URL allowlist.

url.description:
Click-through URL. Carries the ticket plus
`__authn_status=sign_up` (or `sign_in` when the email is
already attached to a `User`) and the org id, so the SDK
knows which flow to start.

Returned for `pending` invitations only. `null` once the
invitation is `revoked` / `accepted` / `expired` since the
ticket can no longer be exchanged.

expires_at.description: Unix ms; `null` for invitations that don't expire.

role.description: Role `key` the recipient receives on accept.

inviter_user_id.description:
Override the recorded inviter. **BAPI only** — FAPI pulls
this from the calling session and rejects requests that set
it explicitly.

notify.description:
When `true`, send the invitation email immediately. Set to
`false` to mint the ticket without sending email.

expires_in_days.description: Days until `expires_at`. `null` for invitations that don't expire.

OrganizationInvitationBulkCreateRequest.description:
Body for `POST /v1/organizations/{org_id}/invitations/bulk`.
Atomic across the whole batch: if any single invitation fails
validation, the entire request is rejected and no rows are
inserted.

OrganizationDomain.description:
A domain attached to an `Organization`. Once verified, drives
domain-based enrollment — depending on `enrollment_mode`, sign-ups
with a matching email get an automatic invitation, an automatic
membership-request suggestion, or nothing at all.

Verification runs through a `Challenge` against
`/v1/organizations/{org}/domains/{id}/challenges`. The proof channel
(DNS TXT or email-to-`affiliation_email_address`) is selected by the
challenge's `strategy` (`domain_dns_txt` or `email_code`).

name.description:
The domain (no scheme, no path) — e.g. `acme.com`. Lowercased
and IDN-normalized on write.

verified.description:
`true` once a verification challenge against this domain has
reached `status: verified`.

current_challenge_id.description:
Pointer to the live `Challenge` if one's in flight against this
domain. `null` when no challenge has been issued yet, the
previous one was answered to a terminal status, or the operator
has rotated the proof token.

enrollment_mode.description:
- `manual_invitation` — domain match has no effect on sign-up;
  members must be invited explicitly.
- `automatic_invitation` — sign-ups with a matching email
  skip the invitation step and join as `default_role`.
- `automatic_suggestion` — sign-ups with a matching email get
  an `OrganizationMembershipRequest` row that an admin must
  approve.

affiliation_email_address.description:
Address used as the `email_code` verification target when the
domain is being verified via the `email_code` challenge
strategy. Always sits at the domain being verified
(e.g. `admin@acme.com`).

total_pending_invitations.description:
Pending `OrganizationInvitation` rows generated by this
domain when `enrollment_mode === 'automatic_invitation'`.

total_pending_suggestions.description:
Pending `OrganizationMembershipRequest` rows generated by
this domain when `enrollment_mode === 'automatic_suggestion'`.

affiliation_email_address.description:
Optional address to use as the `email_code` verification target
when an operator later issues an `email_code` challenge against
this domain. Must sit at the domain being verified.

OrganizationDomainUpdateRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /v1/organizations/{org_id}/domains/{id}`. Only the
enrollment policy is editable post-create; to change the domain
name itself, delete and re-add.

OrganizationMembershipRequest.description:
Pending request from a user with a verified domain match to join
an `Organization`. Created automatically when a user signs up
(or adds a verified email) whose address matches an
`OrganizationDomain` whose `enrollment_mode` is
`automatic_suggestion`.

An admin with `org:sys_memberships:manage` accepts the request
to convert it into an `OrganizationMembership`.

Permission.description:
An atomic capability. Members hold permissions transitively
through their `OrganizationMembership.role`. The
`org:<feature>:<verb>` naming is a convention, not enforced by
schema, so dashboards can list custom permissions cleanly.

System permissions (`is_system: true`) are seeded per environment
at bootstrap and cannot be edited or deleted. Custom permissions
are reserved for v0.3+ — v0.2 ships only the system catalog.

### System permissions catalog (v0.2)

Every environment is seeded with exactly these thirteen system
permissions:

| `key` | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `org:sys_profile:read` | Read org name, slug, image, metadata. |
| `org:sys_profile:manage` | Edit org name, slug, image, metadata. |
| `org:sys_profile:delete` | Delete the org (subject to `admin_delete_enabled`). |
| `org:sys_memberships:read` | List members and pending requests. |
| `org:sys_memberships:manage` | Invite, remove, change roles, accept membership requests. |
| `org:sys_invitations:read` | List outgoing invitations. |
| `org:sys_invitations:manage` | Create, revoke, and resend invitations. |
| `org:sys_domains:read` | List verified domains. |
| `org:sys_domains:manage` | Add, verify, change enrollment mode, delete domains. |
| `org:sys_roles:read` | List roles and their permission assignments. |
| `org:sys_roles:manage` | Create, update, and delete roles; assign permissions. |
| `org:sys_permissions:read` | List available permissions. |
| `org:sys_permissions:manage` | Create and delete custom permissions. |

key.description:
Stable, lowercase, snake-case identifier in the form
`org:<feature>:<verb>`. System permissions live under the
reserved `org:sys_*` prefix.

is_system.description:
`true` for the seeded system catalog above. System
permissions cannot be edited or deleted; only their `name`
and `description` may be localized at render time.

Role.description:
A bundle of permissions assignable to an `OrganizationMembership`.
Each environment is seeded with two default roles at bootstrap
(`org:admin` and `org:member`); custom roles are scoped to the
environment and shared across all its organizations.

### Default roles (v0.2)

| `key` | Permissions | `is_creator_eligible` | `is_default` |
|---|---|:-:|:-:|
| `org:admin` | All thirteen `org:sys_*` system permissions. | `true` | `false` |
| `org:member` | `org:sys_profile:read`, `org:sys_memberships:read`, `org:sys_domains:read` *(read-only subset; v0.2 introduces the implicit `:read` companion to each `sys_*:manage` system permission as a separate seeded row)*. | `false` | `true` |

`is_creator_eligible` controls which roles can be picked as the
org's `creator_role`; `is_default` flags the role assigned to
members enrolled via `automatic_invitation` or accepted
`OrganizationMembershipRequest`.

key.description:
Stable, lowercase, snake-case identifier in the form
`org:<name>`. Defaults: `org:admin`, `org:member`.

is_creator_eligible.description:
Whether this role may be picked as
`Organization.creator_role`. The seeded `org:admin` role is
eligible; `org:member` is not.

is_default.description:
Whether this role is the implicit role for members enrolled
automatically (`automatic_invitation`,
`OrganizationMembershipRequest` accept). Exactly one role per
environment carries `is_default: true`.

is_system.description:
`true` for the seeded `org:admin` and `org:member` rows.
System roles' `permissions[]` cannot be edited; only `name`
and `description` may be customized.

permissions.description:
The role's permission set as a list of `Permission.key`
strings (not full `Permission` objects). Order is stable
(alphabetical) for deterministic comparisons.

RoleCreateRequest.description:
Body for `POST /v1/roles` (BAPI). Custom roles only — system
roles are seeded and not creatable through the API.

key.description:
Custom keys must not collide with the reserved
`org:admin` / `org:member` defaults or anything under the
`org:sys_*` permission prefix.

is_default.description:
Setting this to `true` clears the flag on whichever role
currently holds it (exactly one default per environment).

permissions.description:
The role's permission set as `Permission.key` strings. Each
key must already exist (system or custom) at the time of
write.

RoleUpdateRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /v1/roles/{id}` (BAPI). System roles allow only
`name` / `description` edits; custom roles allow the full set.

RolePermissionsRequest.description:
Body for `PUT /v1/roles/{role_id}/permissions`. Replaces the
role's permission set wholesale — the resulting set is exactly
the keys you send. Pass `[]` to clear all permissions.

permissions.description:
Permission `key` strings. Each must already exist (system or
custom) at the time of write; missing keys return
`422 invalid_field` and the role is not modified.

OauthProvider.description:
Per-environment social-IdP configuration. The SDK reads `OauthProvider`
rows to render `<SocialButtons />`; the server reads them to drive the
`oauth_<provider_key>` first-factor `Challenge` strategy. Three kinds
share a single shape:

- `preset` — bundled IdPs the platform ships with (`google`, `github`,
  `apple`, `microsoft`). The OIDC discovery fields and default scope
  set are filled in by the platform on create; the operator only
  supplies `client_id`, `client_secret`, and any toggles
  (`enabled`, `allow_sign_in`, …).
- `custom_oidc` — workspace-registered OpenID Connect IdP. The
  operator supplies an `issuer` URL; the server fetches and caches
  `<issuer>/.well-known/openid-configuration` and populates the OIDC
  endpoint fields automatically. `discovery_endpoint`,
  `authorization_endpoint`, `token_endpoint`, `userinfo_endpoint`,
  `jwks_uri`, `id_token_signing_algs[]` are read-only when populated
  by discovery, but admin-overridable for self-host edge cases where
  discovery is unreachable.
- `custom_oauth2` — workspace-registered plain OAuth 2.0 IdP without
  OIDC. The operator supplies `authorization_endpoint`,
  `token_endpoint`, and `userinfo_endpoint` directly, plus
  `userinfo_method` (`GET` / `POST`) and `userinfo_auth` (`bearer` /
  `basic` / `query`) so the server knows how to call the userinfo
  endpoint.

### Secret handling

`client_secret` is **write-only**. It's accepted on
`POST /v1/oauth-providers` and `PATCH /v1/oauth-providers/{id}` (see
`OauthProviderRequest`), encrypted at rest, and never returned in any
response. The public payload exposes `client_id` only.

### Computed redirect_uri

`redirect_uri` is computed server-side as
`https://<env_slug>.authn.sh/v1/oauth-callback/<provider_key>` and is
read-only. It's the value the operator must register with the IdP.

### attribute_mapping

Map of authn.sh `User` / `ExternalAccount` fields to IdP claim or
userinfo paths. Presets ship with sensible defaults
(`email_address` → `email`, `first_name` → `given_name`,
`last_name` → `family_name`, …); operators can override per row to
match a non-standard IdP.

### Identifier

ID prefix `oauthp_` (e.g. `oauthp_01HKX9SY9V7H7TF8C8K7J9X4ZA`).

provider_kind.description:
Discriminates which subset of fields applies. Immutable after
create.

provider_key.description:
Stable slug used in the `oauth_<provider_key>` `Challenge.strategy`
and in the computed `redirect_uri`. For `provider_kind: "preset"`
this is one of the bundled values (`google`, `github`, `apple`,
`microsoft`). For `custom_oidc` / `custom_oauth2` it's a
workspace-chosen slug, unique per environment.

enabled.description:
When `false`, the provider is hidden from the SDK and the
`oauth_<provider_key>` strategy is rejected by the FAPI. The row
survives the toggle so existing `ExternalAccount` links remain
valid.

allow_sign_in.description:
When `true`, an unsigned-in user can authenticate with this
provider via the `oauth_<provider_key>` strategy on a `SignIn`.

allow_sign_up.description:
When `true`, a first-time visitor can complete a `SignUp` via
this provider. With `allow_sign_in: true` and `allow_sign_up: false`
the provider is sign-in-only — the callback rejects unknown
identifiers instead of creating a `User`.

block_email_subaddresses.description:
When `true`, addresses with a `+tag` segment returned by the IdP
are refused at sign-up under this provider. Mirrors the
instance-level `restrictions.block_email_subaddresses` knob but
scoped per-provider.

client_id.description:
OAuth 2.0 client identifier issued by the IdP. Public — sent on
every `/authorize` redirect.

scopes.description:
Scope set requested on the `/authorize` redirect. Presets ship
with a sensible default (`openid email profile` for OIDC presets;
`read:user user:email` for GitHub; etc.); operators can add
provider-specific scopes here without spec changes.

additional_authorization_params.description:
Free-form `key=value` pairs merged into the `/authorize` query
string after the standard params (`client_id`, `redirect_uri`,
`scope`, `state`, …). Useful for IdP-specific knobs like Google's
`prompt=select_account` or Microsoft's `tenant=common`.

attribute_mapping.description:
Map of authn.sh field names (keys) to IdP claim or userinfo paths
(values). Keys are the standard `User` / `ExternalAccount` field
names: `email_address`, `first_name`, `last_name`,
`profile_image_url`, `provider_user_id`. Values are dot-separated
JSON paths into the OIDC ID token claims (or userinfo body for
OAuth 2.0).

redirect_uri.description:
Server-computed callback URL the IdP must redirect to after the
authorization code grant. Format:
`https://<env_slug>.authn.sh/v1/oauth-callback/<provider_key>`.
Operators register this exact value with the IdP's developer
console.

issuer.description:
OIDC issuer URL. Required for `provider_kind: "custom_oidc"` (the
server fetches `<issuer>/.well-known/openid-configuration` from
this base). Set by the platform for `preset` rows. `null` for
`custom_oauth2`.

discovery_endpoint.description:
Resolved OIDC discovery document URL. Populated by the server
from `issuer` for `preset` and `custom_oidc` rows; admin-
overridable for self-host edge cases where the standard
`/.well-known/openid-configuration` path is wrong. `null` for
`custom_oauth2`.

authorization_endpoint.description:
`/authorize` URL. Populated by discovery for `preset` /
`custom_oidc`; supplied directly by the operator for
`custom_oauth2`.

token_endpoint.description:
Token exchange URL. Populated by discovery for `preset` /
`custom_oidc`; supplied directly by the operator for
`custom_oauth2`.

userinfo_endpoint.description:
Userinfo URL. Populated by discovery for `preset` /
`custom_oidc`; supplied directly by the operator for
`custom_oauth2`. `null` is allowed for OIDC providers that ship
every required claim in the ID token (the server falls back to
ID-token claims when this is `null`).

jwks_uri.description:
JWKS URL used to verify the OIDC ID token signature. Populated
by discovery for `preset` / `custom_oidc`; admin-overridable.
`null` for `custom_oauth2`.

userinfo_method.description:
HTTP method used to call `userinfo_endpoint`. Required for
`provider_kind: "custom_oauth2"`; `null` for OIDC kinds (those
always use `GET` per the OIDC core spec).

userinfo_auth.description:
How the access token is presented when calling
`userinfo_endpoint`. `bearer` — `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
header. `basic` — HTTP Basic with the access token as the
username. `query` — `?access_token=<token>` query string.
Required for `provider_kind: "custom_oauth2"`; `null` for OIDC
kinds (those always use `bearer`).

OauthProviderRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v1/oauth-providers` and
`PATCH /v1/oauth-providers/{id}`. Mirrors `OauthProvider` minus the
server-computed fields (`id`, `object`, `redirect_uri`, `created_at`,
`updated_at`) and adds the write-only `client_secret`.

### POST vs PATCH

- On `POST`, `provider_kind`, `provider_key`, `name`, and `client_id`
  are required. For `provider_kind: "custom_oidc"` the operator must
  also supply `issuer`. For `provider_kind: "custom_oauth2"` the
  operator must supply `authorization_endpoint`, `token_endpoint`,
  `userinfo_endpoint`, `userinfo_method`, and `userinfo_auth`.
  `client_secret` is required on `POST` for every kind except where
  the IdP does not issue one (rare; PKCE-only flows).
- On `PATCH`, every field is optional and only the keys present in
  the body are updated. `provider_kind` and `provider_key` are
  immutable — sending a different value returns `422`.

### client_secret

Write-only string. Accepted on POST/PATCH, encrypted at rest, and
never returned in any response. Sending an empty string or omitting
the key on PATCH leaves the stored value unchanged. Sending `null`
on PATCH clears the stored secret (e.g. when migrating to a PKCE-
only flow).

provider_kind.description:
Required on `POST`; immutable on `PATCH`. Discriminates which
subset of fields applies on this row.

provider_key.description:
Required on `POST`; immutable on `PATCH`. For `preset` rows must
be one of `google` / `github` / `apple` / `microsoft`.

client_secret.description:
Write-only. Encrypted at rest; never returned by any GET.
Omit on PATCH to leave the stored value untouched; send `null`
to clear it.

scopes.description:
Overrides the preset-default scope set when supplied. Omit on
`POST` for `preset` rows to use the platform-default scopes.

issuer.description:
Required on `POST` for `provider_kind: "custom_oidc"`. The server
runs OIDC discovery from this base and populates the endpoint
fields. Ignored for `custom_oauth2`.

discovery_endpoint.description:
Optional override for `<issuer>/.well-known/openid-configuration`.
Only used by `preset` / `custom_oidc` rows that need a non-
standard discovery URL.

authorization_endpoint.description:
Required on `POST` for `provider_kind: "custom_oauth2"`. Admin
override for OIDC kinds when discovery cannot reach the IdP.

token_endpoint.description:
Required on `POST` for `provider_kind: "custom_oauth2"`. Admin
override for OIDC kinds.

userinfo_endpoint.description:
Required on `POST` for `provider_kind: "custom_oauth2"`. Optional
for OIDC kinds that ship every required claim in the ID token.

userinfo_method.description:
Required on `POST` for `provider_kind: "custom_oauth2"`. `null`
for OIDC kinds.

userinfo_auth.description:
Required on `POST` for `provider_kind: "custom_oauth2"`. `null`
for OIDC kinds.

OauthProviderTestResult.description:
Result of `POST /v1/oauth-providers/{id}/test`. The dashboard uses
this shape to render a per-provider health indicator on the Social
providers configuration page. Always returned with HTTP `200` even
when probes fail — inspect `errors[]` to decide pass/fail.

authorize_url.description:
The fully-formed `/authorize` URL the SDK would redirect a user
to right now (with a synthetic `state` and the registered
`redirect_uri`). Empty string when the server could not build
it (e.g. OIDC discovery refresh failed and no cached endpoint
exists).

userinfo_status.description:
HTTP status code returned by the unauthenticated probe of
`userinfo_endpoint` (the server sends a token-less HEAD/GET and
records what came back). `401` / `403` are healthy — the
endpoint is reachable and refused the missing token. `5xx` and
timeouts surface a problem on the IdP side. `null` when the
provider has no `userinfo_endpoint` (some OIDC IdPs ship every
claim in the ID token).

errors.description:
Collected probe errors. Empty when every check passed. Each
entry uses the standard `Error` shape (`code` is a stable
machine-readable token; SDKs match on it).

errors.long_message: The cached OIDC discovery document is older than 24h and a refresh attempt timed out. Verify the issuer URL and that the discovery endpoint is reachable from authn.sh.

SmsTemplate.description:
Per-environment SMS body customization for one of a fixed set of
outbound message slugs (verification codes, password resets,
invitations). Each environment is seeded with a default row per slug
on creation; operators edit the body or flip `delivered_by_us` to
hand off sending to their own webhook.

### Slugs

- `verification_code` — first-factor sign-up phone verification
  + standalone `PhoneNumber` verification + second-factor SMS MFA.
  The `phone_code` strategy renders this template.
- `reset_password_code` — SMS variant of the password-reset flow
  (when the env's `reset_password` strategy is configured to use
  SMS rather than email). Reserved for v0.5; the row exists in
  v0.4 so operators can prep their copy.
- `invitation` — operator-driven invitation SMS. Mirrors
  `Invitation` (v0.1) but for phone-based invites; reserved for
  v0.5.

### Body templating

`body` is a Liquid-style template with `{{placeholder}}`
interpolation. Standard placeholders:

- `{{otp_code}}` — the 6-digit numeric code (verification +
  reset).
- `{{app.name}}` — the environment's `display_config.application_name`.
- `{{app.url}}` — the environment's `display_config.home_url`.
- `{{user.first_name}}` / `{{user.last_name}}` — when the message
  has a known recipient.
- `{{ttl_minutes}}` — how long the code remains valid (currently
  fixed at 10 minutes for `verification_code`).

Unknown placeholders render as the empty string.

### `delivered_by_us`

When `true` (default), authn.sh's SMS engine sends the message via
the env's `Environment.sms.driver`. When `false`, the engine emits
an `sms.queued` audit-log entry and a webhook event but does not
call any driver — the operator's webhook handler is expected to
take over and send the SMS through their own carrier relationship.

### Identifier

ID prefix `tmpl_` (e.g. `tmpl_01HKX9SY9V7H7TF8C8K7J9X4ZA`). Shared
with future `EmailTemplate` rows.

slug.description:
Stable identifier the SMS engine looks up at send time.
Immutable on the row.

body.description:
Liquid-style template body. The 1600-char ceiling matches the
multi-segment SMS upper bound; messages over 160 chars
auto-split into multiple segments at the carrier.
examples: Your {{app.name}} verification code is {{otp_code}}. It expires in {{ttl_minutes}} minutes.

delivered_by_us.description:
When `true` (the default), authn.sh sends the SMS via the env's
`Environment.sms.driver`. When `false`, the operator handles
sending via webhook — the server emits an `sms.queued` event
but does not call any driver.

from_number_override.description:
Per-template override of `Environment.sms.from_number` in E.164
form. Useful when `verification_code` should send from a
branded short code while `invitation` uses a long-form number.
`null` falls through to the env default.

examples.body: Your {{app.name}} verification code is {{otp_code}}. It expires in {{ttl_minutes}} minutes.
{{user.first_name}}, you've been invited to {{app.name}}. Tap to join: {{app.url}}/accept-invite

SmsTemplateRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /v1/sms-templates/{slug}`. All fields optional —
only the supplied keys are updated. `slug` itself is immutable
(it's the URL parameter); `id`, `created_at`, and `updated_at` are
computed.

delivered_by_us.description:
When `false`, the operator handles sending via webhook —
authn.sh emits the `sms.queued` event but does not call any
driver.

from_number_override.description:
Per-template override of `Environment.sms.from_number` in
E.164. `null` clears the override and falls through to the env
default.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated but not authorized for the action.

NotFound.description: Resource does not exist (or is not visible to the caller).

Conflict.description: State conflict — e.g. an `Idempotency-Key` reuse with a different request body.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until the next token in the bucket.

LimitParam.name: limit
LimitParam.description: Maximum rows to return. Default `10`, maximum `500`.

OffsetParam.name: offset
OffsetParam.description: Rows to skip before returning results.

OrderByParam.name: order_by
OrderByParam.description:
Sort key prefixed with `+` (ascending) or `-` (descending). Per-resource
endpoints document the allowed keys.

IdempotencyKeyHeader.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKeyHeader.description:
Caller-supplied UUID for `POST` mutations. The server caches the response
for 24 hours; replays with the same key return the cached response without
re-executing. A different request body under the same key returns `409`.
